MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE, PER 170.6 C.C.P. to Judge JUDGE SHULMAN AND JUDGE ULMER
Off Calendar Aug-11-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
EFRAIN ARAUZ (Tel: 415-823-2137) as plaintiff's son;
BENJAMIN CASTANEDA as plaintiff's son (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. ALEKSANDR A. VOLKOV, Esq. of Law Offices of Svetlana M. Shirinova (Tel: 415-947-0703) for defendants (via Zoom videoconference).

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MARIA GARCIA'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE, PER 170.6 C.C.P. TO JUDGE SHULMAN AND JUDGE ULMER.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The challenge is denied. A litigant may only peremptorily challenge one judge pursuant to CCP 170.6(a)(2).

Movant improperly attempts to challenge two judges.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Cindy Pacatte, CSR No. 12839, Email: cindyepacatte@gmail.com, Tel: 707-480-8360 (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-11-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
EFRAIN ARAUZ (Tel: 415-823-2137) as plaintiff's son;
BENJAMIN CASTANEDA as plaintiff's son (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. ALEKSANDR A. VOLKOV, Esq. of Law Offices of Svetlana M. Shirinova (Tel: 415-947-0703) for defendants (via Zoom videoconference).

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS ALEKSANDR VOLKOV, SVETLANA SHIRINOVA, CONCEPCION HERNANDEZ, JESUS VALLE, AND ALEXIS MORENO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' unopposed anti-SLAPP motion to strike the complaint is granted. The court dismisses this case. Defendants demonstrate that this case arises from petitioning activity (prong one). (See Bergstein v. Stroock & Stroock & Lavan LLP (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 793, 811 [suit against attorneys representing adversary implicates CCP 425.16].) Plaintiff failed to oppose this motion and presented no evidence to demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing (prong two). The motion is therefore granted. Plaintiff filed an amended complaint. But a party cannot file an amended complaint to avoid a pending anti-SLAPP motion. (See JKC3H8 v. Colton (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 468, 477-478 ["A plaintiff or cross-complainant may not seek to subvert or avoid a ruling on an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the challenged complaint or cross-complaint in response to the motion."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Cindy Pacatte, CSR No. 12839, Email: cindyepacatte@gmail.com, Tel: 707-480-8360 (302/RBU)
